17: // ACL provides an entry point into the permissions engine for determining if an
18: // action is allowed on a resource based on a principal's (user or group) grants.
19: type ACL struct {
20: 	scopeMap map[string][]Grant
21: }
22: 
23: // ACLResults provides a type for the permission's engine results so that we can
24: // pass more detailed information along in the future if we want. It was useful
25: // in Vault, may be useful here.
26: type ACLResults struct {
27: 	AuthenticationFinished bool
28: 	Authorized             bool
29: 	OutputFields           OutputFieldsMap
30: 
31: 	// This is included but unexported for testing/debugging
32: 	scopeMap map[string][]Grant
33: }
34: 
35: // Permission provides information about the specific
36: // resources that a user has been granted access to for a given scope, resource, and action.
37: type Permission struct {
38: 	ScopeId  string // The scope id for which the permission applies.
39: 	Resource resource.Type
40: 	Action   action.Type
41: 
42: 	ResourceIds []string // Any specific resource ids that have been referred in the grant's `id` field, if applicable.
43: 	OnlySelf    bool     // The grant only allows actions against the user's own resources.
44: 	All         bool     // We got a wildcard in the grant string's `id` field.
45: }
46: 
47: // UserPermissions is a set of Permissions for a User.
48: type UserPermissions struct {
49: 	UserId      string
50: 	Permissions []Permission
51: }
52: 
53: // Resource defines something within boundary that requires authorization
54: // capabilities. Resources must have a ScopeId.
55: type Resource struct {
56: 	// ScopeId is the scope that contains the Resource.
57: 	ScopeId string `json:"scope_id,omitempty"`
58: 
59: 	// Id is the public id of the resource.
60: 	Id string `json:"id,omitempty"`
61: 
62: 	// Type of resource.
63: 	Type resource.Type `json:"type,omitempty"`
64: 
65: 	// Pin if defined would constrain the resource within the collection of the
66: 	// pin id.
67: 	Pin string `json:"pin,omitempty"`
68: }

70: // NewACL creates an ACL from the grants provided.
71: func NewACL(grants ...Grant) ACL {
72: 	ret := ACL{
73: 		scopeMap: make(map[string][]Grant, len(grants)),
74: 	}
75: 
76: 	for _, grant := range grants {
77: 		ret.scopeMap[grant.scope.Id] = append(ret.scopeMap[grant.scope.Id], grant)
78: 	}
79: 
80: 	return ret
81: }
82: 
83: // Allowed determines if the grants for an ACL allow an action for a resource.
84: func (a ACL) Allowed(r Resource, aType action.Type, userId string, opt ...Option) (results ACLResults) {
85: 	opts := getOpts(opt...)
86: 
87: 	// First, get the grants within the specified scope
88: 	grants := a.scopeMap[r.ScopeId]
89: 	results.scopeMap = a.scopeMap
90: 
91: 	var parentAction action.Type
92: 	split := strings.Split(aType.String(), ":")
93: 	if len(split) == 2 {
94: 		parentAction = action.Map[split[0]]
95: 	}
96: 	// Now, go through and check the cases indicated above
97: 	for _, grant := range grants {
98: 		var outputFieldsOnly bool
99: 		switch {
100: 		case len(grant.actions) == 0:
101: 			// Continue with the next grant, unless we have output fields
102: 			// specified in which case we continue to be able to apply the
103: 			// output fields depending on ID and type.
104: 			if len(grant.OutputFields) > 0 {
105: 				outputFieldsOnly = true
106: 			} else {
107: 				continue
108: 			}
109: 		case grant.actions[aType]:
110: 			// We have this action
111: 		case grant.actions[parentAction]:
112: 			// We don't have this action, but it's a subaction and we have the
113: 			// parent action. As an example, if we are looking for "read:self"
114: 			// and have "read", this is sufficient.
115: 		case grant.actions[action.All]:
116: 			// All actions are allowed
117: 		default:
118: 			// No actions in the grant match what we're looking for, so continue
119: 			// with the next grant
120: 			continue
121: 		}
122: 
123: 		// We step through all grants, to fetch the full list of output fields.
124: 		// However, we shortcut if we find *.
125: 		//
126: 		// If the action was not found above but we did find output fields in
127: 		// patterns that match, we do not authorize the request, but we do build
128: 		// up the output fields patterns.
129: 		//
130: 		// Note that when using IsActionOrParent it is merely to test whether it
131: 		// is an allowed format since some formats operate ony on collections
132: 		// (or don't operate at all on collections) and we want to ensure that
133: 		// it is/isn't a create or list command or subcommand to know whether
134: 		// that form is valid. The actual checking of whether the given action
135: 		// is granted to the user already happened above.
136: 		var found bool
137: 		switch {
138: 		// Case 1: We only allow specific actions on specific types for the
139: 		// anonymous user. ID being supplied or not doesn't matter in this case,
140: 		// it must be an explicit type and action(s); adding this as an explicit
141: 		// case here prevents duplicating logic in two of the other more
142: 		// general-purpose cases below (3 and 4). See notes there about ID being
143: 		// present or not.
144: 		case !opts.withSkipAnonymousUserRestrictions &&
145: 			(userId == AnonymousUserId || userId == ""):
146: 			switch {
147: 			// Allow discovery of scopes, so that auth methods within can be
148: 			// discovered
149: 			case grant.typ == r.Type &&
150: 				grant.typ == resource.Scope &&
151: 				(aType == action.List || aType == action.NoOp):
152: 				found = true
153: 
154: 			// Allow discovery of and authenticating to auth methods
155: 			case grant.typ == r.Type &&
156: 				grant.typ == resource.AuthMethod &&
157: 				(aType == action.List || aType == action.NoOp || aType == action.Authenticate):
158: 				found = true
159: 			}
160: 
161: 		// Case 2:
162: 		// id=<resource.id>;actions=<action> where ID cannot be a wildcard; or
163: 		// id=<resource.id>;output_fields=<fields> where fields cannot be a
164: 		// wildcard.
165: 		case grant.id == r.Id &&
166: 			grant.id != "" &&
167: 			grant.id != "*" &&
168: 			grant.typ == resource.Unknown &&
169: 			!action.List.IsActionOrParent(aType) &&
170: 			!action.Create.IsActionOrParent(aType):
171: 
172: 			found = true
173: 
174: 		// Case 3: type=<resource.type>;actions=<action> when action is list or
175: 		// create. Must be a top level collection, otherwise must be one of the
176: 		// two formats specified in cases 4 or 5. Or,
177: 		// type=resource.type;output_fields=<fields> and no action. This is more
178: 		// of a semantic difference compared to 4 more than a security
179: 		// difference; this type is for clarity as it ties more closely to the
180: 		// concept of create and list as actions on a collection, operating on a
181: 		// collection directly. The format in case 4 will still work for
182: 		// create/list on collections but that's more of a shortcut to allow
183: 		// things like id=*;type=*;actions=* for admin flows so that you don't
184: 		// need to separate out explicit collection actions into separate typed
185: 		// grants for each collection within a role. This does mean there are
186: 		// "two ways of doing things" but it's a reasonable UX tradeoff given
187: 		// that "all IDs" can reasonably be construed to include "and the one
188: 		// I'm making" and "all of them for listing".
189: 		case grant.id == "" &&
190: 			r.Id == "" &&
191: 			grant.typ == r.Type &&
192: 			grant.typ != resource.Unknown &&
193: 			topLevelType(r.Type) &&
194: 			(action.List.IsActionOrParent(aType) ||
195: 				action.Create.IsActionOrParent(aType)):
196: 
197: 			found = true
198: 
199: 		// Case 4:
200: 		// id=*;type=<resource.type>;actions=<action> where type cannot be
201: 		// unknown but can be a wildcard to allow any resource at all; or
202: 		// id=*;type=<resource.type>;output_fields=<fields> with no action.
203: 		case grant.id == "*" &&
204: 			grant.typ != resource.Unknown &&
205: 			(grant.typ == r.Type ||
206: 				grant.typ == resource.All):
207: 
208: 			found = true
209: 
210: 		// Case 5:
211: 		// id=<pin>;type=<resource.type>;actions=<action> where type can be a
212: 		// wildcard and this this is operating on a non-top-level type. Same for
213: 		// output fields only.
214: 		case grant.id != "" &&
215: 			grant.id == r.Pin &&
216: 			grant.typ != resource.Unknown &&
217: 			(grant.typ == r.Type || grant.typ == resource.All) &&
218: 			!topLevelType(r.Type):
219: 
220: 			found = true
221: 		}
222: 
223: 		if found {
224: 			if !outputFieldsOnly {
225: 				results.Authorized = true
226: 			}
227: 			if results.OutputFields = results.OutputFields.AddFields(grant.OutputFields.Fields()); results.OutputFields.HasAll() && results.Authorized {
228: 				return
229: 			}
230: 		}
231: 	}
232: 	return
233: }
234: 
235: // ListPermissions builds a set of Permissions based on the grants in the ACL.
236: // Permissions are determined for the given resource for each of the provided scopes.
237: // There must be a grant for a given resource for one of the provided "id actions"
238: // or for action.All in order for a Permission to be created for the scope.
239: // The set of "id actions" is resource dependant, but will generally include all
240: // actions that can be taken on an individual resource.
241: func (a ACL) ListPermissions(requestedScopes map[string]*scopes.ScopeInfo,
242: 	requestedType resource.Type,
243: 	idActions action.ActionSet,
244: 	userId string,
245: ) []Permission {
246: 	perms := make([]Permission, 0, len(requestedScopes))
247: 	for scopeId := range requestedScopes {
248: 		p := Permission{
249: 			ScopeId:  scopeId,
250: 			Resource: requestedType,
251: 			Action:   action.List,
252: 			OnlySelf: true, // default to only self to be restrictive
253: 		}
254: 		if userId == RecoveryUserId {
255: 			p.All = true
256: 			p.OnlySelf = false
257: 			perms = append(perms, p)
258: 			continue
259: 		}
260: 
261: 		// Get grants for a specific scope id from the source of truth.
262: 		grants := a.scopeMap[scopeId]
263: 		for _, grant := range grants {
264: 			// This grant doesn't match what we're looking for, ignore.
265: 			if grant.typ != requestedType && grant.typ != resource.All {
266: 				continue
267: 			}
268: 
269: 			// We found a grant that matches the requested resource type:
270: 			// Search to see if one or all actions in the action set have been granted.
271: 			found := false
272: 			if ok := grant.actions[action.All]; ok {
273: 				found = true
274: 			} else {
275: 				for _, a := range idActions {
276: 					if ok := grant.actions[a]; ok {
277: 						found = true
278: 						break
279: 					}
280: 				}
281: 			}
282: 			if !found { // In this case, none of the requested actions were granted for the given scope id.
283: 				continue
284: 			}
285: 
286: 			actions, _ := grant.Actions()
287: 			excludeList := make(action.ActionSet, 0, len(actions))
288: 			for _, aa := range actions {
289: 				if aa != action.List {
290: 					excludeList = append(excludeList, aa)
291: 				}
292: 			}
293: 			p.OnlySelf = p.OnlySelf && excludeList.OnlySelf()
294: 
295: 			switch grant.id {
296: 			case "*":
297: 				p.All = true
298: 			case "":
299: 				continue
300: 			default:
301: 				p.ResourceIds = append(p.ResourceIds, grant.id)
302: 			}
303: 		}
304: 
305: 		if p.All || len(p.ResourceIds) > 0 {
306: 			perms = append(perms, p)
307: 		}
308: 	}
309: 
310: 	return perms
311: }
312: 
313: func topLevelType(typ resource.Type) bool {
314: 	switch typ {
315: 	case resource.AuthMethod,
316: 		resource.AuthToken,
317: 		resource.CredentialStore,
318: 		resource.Group,
319: 		resource.HostCatalog,
320: 		resource.Role,
321: 		resource.Scope,
322: 		resource.Session,
323: 		resource.Target,
324: 		resource.User,
325: 		resource.Worker:
326: 		return true
327: 	}
328: 	return false
329: }

20: // CreateScope will create a scope in the repository and return the written
21: // scope. Supported options include: WithPublicId and WithRandomReader.
22: func (r *Repository) CreateScope(ctx context.Context, s *Scope, userId string, opt ...Option) (*Scope, error) {
23: 	const op = "iam.(Repository).CreateScope"
24: 	if s == nil {
25: 		return nil, errors.New(ctx, errors.InvalidParameter, op, "missing scope")
26: 	}
27: 	if s.Scope == nil {
28: 		return nil, errors.New(ctx, errors.InvalidParameter, op, "missing scope store")
29: 	}
30: 	if s.PublicId != "" {
31: 		return nil, errors.New(ctx, errors.InvalidParameter, op, "public id not empty")
32: 	}
33: 
34: 	var parentOplogWrapper wrapping.Wrapper
35: 	var err error
36: 	switch s.Type {
37: 	case scope.Unknown.String():
38: 		return nil, errors.New(ctx, errors.InvalidParameter, op, "unknown type")
39: 	case scope.Global.String():
40: 		return nil, errors.New(ctx, errors.InvalidParameter, op, "invalid type")
41: 	default:
42: 		switch s.ParentId {
43: 		case "":
44: 			return nil, errors.New(ctx, errors.InvalidParameter, op, "missing parent id")
45: 		case scope.Global.String():
46: 			parentOplogWrapper, err = r.kms.GetWrapper(ctx, scope.Global.String(), kms.KeyPurposeOplog)
47: 		default:
48: 			parentOplogWrapper, err = r.kms.GetWrapper(ctx, s.ParentId, kms.KeyPurposeOplog)
49: 		}
50: 	}
51: 	if err != nil {
52: 		return nil, errors.New(ctx, errors.InvalidParameter, op, "unable to get oplog wrapper")
53: 	}
54: 
55: 	opts := getOpts(opt...)
56: 
57: 	var scopePublicId string
58: 	var scopeMetadata oplog.Metadata
59: 	var scopeRaw any
60: 	{
61: 		scopeType := scope.Map[s.Type]
62: 		if opts.withPublicId != "" {
63: 			if !strings.HasPrefix(opts.withPublicId, scopeType.Prefix()+"_") {
64: 				return nil, errors.New(ctx, errors.InvalidParameter, op, fmt.Sprintf("passed-in public ID %q has wrong prefix for type %q which uses prefix %q", opts.withPublicId, scopeType.String(), scopeType.Prefix()))
65: 			}
66: 			scopePublicId = opts.withPublicId
67: 		} else {
68: 			scopePublicId, err = newScopeId(scopeType)
69: 			if err != nil {
70: 				return nil, errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op)
71: 			}
72: 		}
73: 		sc := s.Clone().(*Scope)
74: 		sc.PublicId = scopePublicId
75: 		scopeRaw = sc
76: 		scopeMetadata, err = r.stdMetadata(ctx, sc)
77: 		if err != nil {
78: 			return nil, errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op)
79: 		}
80: 		scopeMetadata["op-type"] = []string{oplog.OpType_OP_TYPE_CREATE.String()}
81: 	}
82: 
83: 	var adminRolePublicId string
84: 	var adminRoleMetadata oplog.Metadata
85: 	var adminRole *Role
86: 	var adminRoleRaw any

88: 	case userId == "",

90: 		userId == perms.AnyAuthenticatedUserId,
91: 		userId == perms.RecoveryUserId,

106: 		}

116: 			"resource-public-id": []string{adminRolePublicId},

135: 			return nil, errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op, errors.WithMsg("error generating public id for new default role"))
136: 		}

183: 			if err := txnKms.CreateKeys(ctx, s.PublicId, kms.WithRandomReader(reader), kms.WithReaderWriter(dbr, w)); err != nil {
184: 				return errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op, errors.WithMsg("error creating scope keys"))
185: 			}
186: 			childOplogWrapper, err := txnKms.GetWrapper(ctx, s.PublicId, kms.KeyPurposeOplog)
187: 			if err != nil {

190: 
191: 			// We create a new role, then set grants and principals on it. This

380: // UpdateScope will update a scope in the repository and return the written

383: // included in fieldMask. Name and Description are the only updatable fields,

385: // fieldMaskPaths, then an error is returned.
386: func (r *Repository) UpdateScope(ctx context.Context, scope *Scope, version uint32, fieldMaskPaths []string, _ ...Option) (*Scope, int, error) {

394: 	if contains(fieldMaskPaths, "ParentId") {
395: 		return nil, db.NoRowsAffected, errors.New(ctx, errors.InvalidFieldMask, op, "you cannot change a scope's parent")
396: 	}
397: 	var dbMask, nullFields []string
398: 	dbMask, nullFields = dbw.BuildUpdatePaths(
399: 		map[string]any{

401: 			"description":         scope.Description,
402: 			"PrimaryAuthMethodId": scope.PrimaryAuthMethodId, // gorm: it's important that the field start with a capital letter.
403: 		},
404: 		fieldMaskPaths,
405: 		nil,
406: 	)
407: 	// nada to update, so reload scope from db and return it
408: 	if len(dbMask) == 0 && len(nullFields) == 0 {
409: 		return nil, db.NoRowsAffected, errors.E(ctx, errors.WithCode(errors.EmptyFieldMask), errors.WithOp(op))
410: 	}
411: 	resource, rowsUpdated, err := r.update(ctx, scope, version, dbMask, nullFields)
412: 	if err != nil {
413: 		if errors.IsUniqueError(err) {
414: 			return nil, db.NoRowsAffected, errors.New(ctx, errors.NotUnique, op, fmt.Sprintf("%s name %s already exists", scope.PublicId, scope.Name))
415: 		}
416: 		return nil, db.NoRowsAffected, errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op, errors.WithMsg(fmt.Sprintf("for public id %s", scope.PublicId)))
417: 	}

432: 			return nil, nil
433: 		}

447: 	}

122: //
123: // This is being added for a quick turnaround purpose and to avoid making large
124: // numbers of changes to tests when we may do a much bigger refactor; when those
125: // items are addressed this can be removed.
126: func NewVerifierContextWithAccounts(ctx context.Context,
127: 	iamRepoFn common.IamRepoFactory,
128: 	authTokenRepoFn common.AuthTokenRepoFactory,
129: 	serversRepoFn common.ServersRepoFactory,
130: 	passwordAuthRepoFn common.PasswordAuthRepoFactory,
131: 	oidcAuthRepoFn common.OidcAuthRepoFactory,
132: 	kms *kms.Kms,
133: 	requestInfo *authpb.RequestInfo,
134: ) context.Context {
135: 	return context.WithValue(ctx, verifierKey, &verifier{
136: 		iamRepoFn:          iamRepoFn,
137: 		authTokenRepoFn:    authTokenRepoFn,
138: 		serversRepoFn:      serversRepoFn,
139: 		passwordAuthRepoFn: passwordAuthRepoFn,
140: 		oidcAuthRepoFn:     oidcAuthRepoFn,
141: 		kms:                kms,
142: 		requestInfo:        requestInfo,
143: 	})
144: }
145: 
146: // NewVerifierContext creates a context that carries a verifier object from the
147: // HTTP handlers to the gRPC service handlers. It should only be created in the
148: // HTTP handler and should exist for every request that reaches the service
149: // handlers.
150: func NewVerifierContext(ctx context.Context,
151: 	iamRepoFn common.IamRepoFactory,
152: 	authTokenRepoFn common.AuthTokenRepoFactory,
153: 	serversRepoFn common.ServersRepoFactory,
154: 	kms *kms.Kms,
155: 	requestInfo *authpb.RequestInfo,
156: ) context.Context {
157: 	return NewVerifierContextWithAccounts(ctx, iamRepoFn, authTokenRepoFn, serversRepoFn, nil, nil, kms, requestInfo)
158: }
159: 
160: // Verify takes in a context that has expected parameters as values and runs an
161: // authn/authz check. It returns a user ID, the scope ID for the request (which
162: // may come from the URL and may come from the token) and whether or not to
163: // proceed, e.g. whether the authn/authz check resulted in failure. If an error
164: // occurs it's logged to the system log.
165: func Verify(ctx context.Context, opt ...Option) (ret VerifyResults) {
166: 	const op = "auth.Verify"
167: 	ret.Error = handlers.ForbiddenError()
168: 	v, ok := ctx.Value(verifierKey).(*verifier)
169: 	if !ok {
170: 		// We don't have a logger yet and this should never happen in any
171: 		// context we won't catch in tests
172: 		panic("no verifier information found in context")
173: 	}
174: 
175: 	ret.v = v
176: 
177: 	v.ctx = ctx
178: 
179: 	ea := &event.Auth{}
180: 	defer event.WriteAudit(ctx, op, event.WithAuth(ea))
181: 
182: 	opts := getOpts(opt...)
183: 
184: 	ret.Scope = new(scopes.ScopeInfo)
185: 
186: 	// Note: we don't call RequestContextFromCtx here because that performs a
187: 	// SelfOrDefault. That's useful in the handlers, but here we don't want to
188: 	// do anything if it's nil. That's mainly useful in tests where
189: 	// DisableAuthEntirely is set. Later, if we don't have the value set at all,
190: 	// we have some safeguards to ensure we fail safe (e.g. no output fields at
191: 	// all). So it provides a good indication as well whether we have set this
192: 	// where and when needed.
193: 	var reqInfo *requests.RequestContext
194: 	reqInfoRaw := ctx.Value(requests.ContextRequestInformationKey)
195: 	if reqInfoRaw != nil {
196: 		reqInfo = reqInfoRaw.(*requests.RequestContext)
197: 	}
198: 
199: 	// In tests we often simply disable auth so we can test the service handlers
200: 	// without fuss
201: 	if v.requestInfo.DisableAuthEntirely {
202: 		yes := true
203: 		ea.DisabledAuthEntirely = &yes
204: 		const op = "auth.(disabled).lookupScope"
205: 		ret.Scope.Id = v.requestInfo.ScopeIdOverride
206: 		if ret.Scope.Id == "" {
207: 			ret.Scope.Id = opts.withScopeId
208: 		}
209: 		// Look up scope details to return. We can skip a lookup when using the
210: 		// global scope
211: 		switch ret.Scope.Id {
212: 		case scope.Global.String():
213: 			ret.Scope = &scopes.ScopeInfo{
214: 				Id:            scope.Global.String(),
215: 				Type:          scope.Global.String(),
216: 				Name:          scope.Global.String(),
217: 				Description:   "Global Scope",
218: 				ParentScopeId: "",
219: 			}
220: 
221: 		default:
222: 			iamRepo, err := v.iamRepoFn()
223: 			if err != nil {
224: 				ret.Error = errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op, errors.WithMsg("failed to get iam repo"))
225: 				return
226: 			}
227: 
228: 			scp, err := iamRepo.LookupScope(v.ctx, ret.Scope.Id)
229: 			if err != nil {
230: 				ret.Error = errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op)
231: 				return
232: 			}
233: 			if scp == nil {
234: 				ret.Error = errors.New(ctx, errors.InvalidParameter, op, fmt.Sprintf("non-existent scope %q", ret.Scope.Id))
235: 				return
236: 			}
237: 			ret.Scope = &scopes.ScopeInfo{
238: 				Id:            scp.GetPublicId(),
239: 				Type:          scp.GetType(),
240: 				Name:          scp.GetName(),
241: 				Description:   scp.GetDescription(),
242: 				ParentScopeId: scp.GetParentId(),
243: 			}
244: 		}
245: 		ret.UserId = v.requestInfo.UserIdOverride
246: 		ret.UserData.User.Id = util.Pointer(ret.UserId)
247: 		if reqInfo != nil {
248: 			reqInfo.UserId = ret.UserId
249: 		}
250: 		ea.UserInfo = &event.UserInfo{UserId: ret.UserId}
251: 		ret.Error = nil
252: 		return
253: 	}
254: 
255: 	v.act = opts.withAction
256: 	v.res = &perms.Resource{
257: 		ScopeId: opts.withScopeId,
258: 		Id:      opts.withId,
259: 		Pin:     opts.withPin,
260: 		Type:    opts.withType,
261: 	}
262: 	// Global scope has no parent ID; account for this
263: 	if opts.withId == scope.Global.String() && opts.withType == resource.Scope {
264: 		v.res.ScopeId = scope.Global.String()
265: 	}
266: 
267: 	if v.requestInfo.EncryptedToken != "" {
268: 		v.decryptToken(ctx)
269: 	}
270: 
271: 	var authResults perms.ACLResults
272: 	var grantTuples []perms.GrantTuple
273: 	var userData template.Data
274: 	var err error
275: 	authResults, ret.UserData, ret.Scope, v.acl, grantTuples, err = v.performAuthCheck(ctx)
276: 	if err != nil {
277: 		event.WriteError(ctx, op, err, event.WithInfoMsg("error performing authn/authz check"))
278: 		return
279: 	}
280: 
281: 	if ret.UserData.User.Id != nil {
282: 		ret.UserId = *ret.UserData.User.Id
283: 	}
284: 	ret.AuthTokenId = v.requestInfo.PublicId
285: 	ret.AuthenticationFinished = authResults.AuthenticationFinished
286: 	if !authResults.Authorized {
287: 		if v.requestInfo.DisableAuthzFailures {
288: 			ret.Error = nil
289: 			// TODO: Decide whether to remove this
290: 			err := event.WriteObservation(ctx, op,
291: 				event.WithHeader(
292: 					"auth-results", struct {
293: 						Msg      string          `json:"msg"`
294: 						Resource *perms.Resource `json:"resource"`
295: 						UserId   string          `json:"user_id"`
296: 						Action   string          `json:"action"`
297: 					}{
298: 						Msg:      "failed authz info for request",
299: 						Resource: v.res,
300: 						UserId:   ret.UserId,
301: 						Action:   v.act.String(),
302: 					}))
303: 			if err != nil {
304: 				event.WriteError(ctx, op, err)
305: 			}
306: 		} else {
307: 			// If the anon user was used (either no token, or invalid (perhaps
308: 			// expired) token), return a 401. That way if it's an authn'd user
309: 			// that is not authz'd we'll return 403 to be explicit.
310: 			if ret.UserId == AnonymousUserId {
311: 				ret.Error = handlers.UnauthenticatedError()
312: 			}
313: 			ea.UserInfo = &event.UserInfo{
314: 				UserId: ret.UserId,
315: 			}
316: 			return
317: 		}
318: 	}
319: 
320: 	grants := make([]event.Grant, 0, len(grantTuples))
321: 	for _, g := range grantTuples {
322: 		grants = append(grants, event.Grant{
323: 			Grant:   g.Grant,
324: 			RoleId:  g.RoleId,
325: 			ScopeId: g.ScopeId,
326: 		})
327: 	}
328: 	ea.UserInfo = &event.UserInfo{
329: 		UserId: ret.UserId,
330: 	}
331: 	if userData.Account.Id != nil {
332: 		ea.UserInfo.AuthAccountId = *userData.Account.Id
333: 	}
334: 	ea.GrantsInfo = &event.GrantsInfo{
335: 		Grants: grants,
336: 	}
337: 	if userData.User.FullName != nil {
338: 		ea.UserName = *userData.User.FullName
339: 	}
340: 	if userData.User.Email != nil {
341: 		ea.UserEmail = *userData.User.Email
342: 	}
343: 
344: 	if reqInfo != nil {
345: 		reqInfo.UserId = ret.UserId
346: 		reqInfo.OutputFields = authResults.OutputFields
347: 	}
348: 
349: 	ret.Error = nil
350: 	return
351: }
352: 
353: func (v *verifier) decryptToken(ctx context.Context) {
354: 	const op = "auth.(verifier).decryptToken"
355: 	switch v.requestInfo.TokenFormat {
356: 	case uint32(AuthTokenTypeUnknown):
357: 		// Nothing to decrypt
358: 		return
359: 
360: 	case uint32(AuthTokenTypeBearer), uint32(AuthTokenTypeSplitCookie):
361: 		if v.kms == nil {
362: 			event.WriteError(ctx, op, stderrors.New("no KMS object available to authz system"))
363: 			v.requestInfo.TokenFormat = uint32(AuthTokenTypeUnknown)
364: 			return
365: 		}
366: 
367: 		tokenRepo, err := v.authTokenRepoFn()
368: 		if err != nil {
369: 			event.WriteError(ctx, op, err, event.WithInfoMsg("failed to get authtoken repo"))
370: 			v.requestInfo.TokenFormat = uint32(AuthTokenTypeUnknown)
371: 			return
372: 		}
373: 
374: 		at, err := tokenRepo.LookupAuthToken(v.ctx, v.requestInfo.PublicId)
375: 		if err != nil {
376: 			event.WriteError(ctx, op, err, event.WithInfoMsg("failed to look up auth token by public ID"))
377: 			v.requestInfo.TokenFormat = uint32(AuthTokenTypeUnknown)
378: 			return
379: 		}
380: 		if at == nil {
381: 			event.WriteError(ctx, op, stderrors.New("nil result from looking up auth token by public ID"))
382: 			v.requestInfo.TokenFormat = uint32(AuthTokenTypeUnknown)
383: 			return
384: 		}
385: 
386: 		tokenWrapper, err := v.kms.GetWrapper(v.ctx, at.GetScopeId(), kms.KeyPurposeTokens)
387: 		if err != nil {
388: 			event.WriteError(ctx, op, err, event.WithInfoMsg("unable to get wrapper for tokens; continuing as anonymous user"))
389: 			v.requestInfo.TokenFormat = uint32(AuthTokenTypeUnknown)
390: 			return
391: 		}
392: 
393: 		version := v.requestInfo.EncryptedToken[0:len(globals.ServiceTokenV1)]
394: 		switch version {
395: 		case globals.ServiceTokenV1:
396: 		default:
397: 			event.WriteError(ctx, op, stderrors.New("unknown token encryption version; continuing as anonymous user"), event.WithInfo("version", version))
398: 			v.requestInfo.TokenFormat = uint32(AuthTokenTypeUnknown)
399: 			return
400: 		}
401: 		marshaledToken, err := base58.FastBase58Decoding(v.requestInfo.EncryptedToken[len(globals.ServiceTokenV1):])
402: 		if err != nil {
403: 			event.WriteError(ctx, op, err, event.WithInfoMsg("error unmarshaling base58 token; continuing as anonymous user"))
404: 			v.requestInfo.TokenFormat = uint32(AuthTokenTypeUnknown)
405: 			return
406: 		}
407: 
408: 		blobInfo := new(wrapping.BlobInfo)
409: 		if err := proto.Unmarshal(marshaledToken, blobInfo); err != nil {
410: 			event.WriteError(ctx, op, err, event.WithInfoMsg("error decoding encrypted token; continuing as anonymous user"))
411: 			v.requestInfo.TokenFormat = uint32(AuthTokenTypeUnknown)
412: 			return
413: 		}
414: 
415: 		s1Bytes, err := tokenWrapper.Decrypt(v.ctx, blobInfo, wrapping.WithAad([]byte(v.requestInfo.PublicId)))
416: 		if err != nil {
417: 			event.WriteError(ctx, op, err, event.WithInfoMsg("error decrypting encrypted token; continuing as anonymous user"))
418: 			v.requestInfo.TokenFormat = uint32(AuthTokenTypeUnknown)
419: 			return
420: 		}
421: 
422: 		var s1Info tokens.S1TokenInfo
423: 		if err := proto.Unmarshal(s1Bytes, &s1Info); err != nil {
424: 			event.WriteError(ctx, op, err, event.WithInfoMsg("error unmarshaling token info; continuing as anonymous user"))
425: 			v.requestInfo.TokenFormat = uint32(AuthTokenTypeUnknown)
426: 			return
427: 		}
428: 
429: 		if v.requestInfo.TokenFormat == uint32(AuthTokenTypeUnknown) || s1Info.Token == "" || v.requestInfo.PublicId == "" {
430: 			event.WriteError(ctx, op, stderrors.New("after parsing, could not find valid token; continuing as anonymous user"))
431: 			v.requestInfo.TokenFormat = uint32(AuthTokenTypeUnknown)
432: 			return
433: 		}
434: 
435: 		v.requestInfo.Token = s1Info.Token
436: 		return
437: 
438: 	case uint32(AuthTokenTypeRecoveryKms):
439: 		if v.kms == nil {
440: 			event.WriteError(ctx, op, stderrors.New("no KMS object available to authz system"))
441: 			v.requestInfo.TokenFormat = uint32(AuthTokenTypeUnknown)
442: 			return
443: 		}
444: 		wrapper := v.kms.GetExternalWrappers(ctx).Recovery()
445: 		if wrapper == nil {
446: 			event.WriteError(ctx, op, stderrors.New("no recovery KMS is available"))
447: 			v.requestInfo.TokenFormat = uint32(AuthTokenTypeUnknown)
448: 			return
449: 		}
450: 		info, err := recovery.ParseRecoveryToken(v.ctx, wrapper, v.requestInfo.EncryptedToken)
451: 		if err != nil {
452: 			event.WriteError(ctx, op, err, event.WithInfoMsg("decrypt recovery token: error parsing and validating recovery token"))
453: 			v.requestInfo.TokenFormat = uint32(AuthTokenTypeUnknown)
454: 			return
455: 		}
456: 		// If we add the validity period to the creation time (which we've
457: 		// verified is before the current time, with a minute of fudging), and
458: 		// it's before now, it's expired and might be a replay.
459: 		if info.CreationTime.Add(globals.RecoveryTokenValidityPeriod).Before(time.Now()) {
460: 			event.WriteError(ctx, op, stderrors.New("recovery token has expired (possible replay attack)"))
461: 			v.requestInfo.TokenFormat = uint32(AuthTokenTypeUnknown)
462: 			return
463: 		}
464: 		repo, err := v.serversRepoFn()
465: 		if err != nil {
466: 			event.WriteError(ctx, op, err, event.WithInfoMsg("decrypt recovery token: error fetching server repo"))
467: 			v.requestInfo.TokenFormat = uint32(AuthTokenTypeUnknown)
468: 			return
469: 		}
470: 		if err := repo.AddNonce(v.ctx, info.Nonce, server.NoncePurposeRecovery); err != nil {
471: 			event.WriteError(ctx, op, err, event.WithInfoMsg("decrypt recovery token: error adding nonce to database (possible replay attack)"))
472: 			v.requestInfo.TokenFormat = uint32(AuthTokenTypeUnknown)
473: 			return
474: 		}
475: 		event.WriteError(ctx, op, stderrors.New("recovery KMS was used to authorize a call"), event.WithInfo("url", v.requestInfo.Path, "method", v.requestInfo.Method))
476: 	}
477: }
478: 
479: func (v verifier) performAuthCheck(ctx context.Context) (
480: 	aclResults perms.ACLResults,
481: 	userData template.Data,
482: 	scopeInfo *scopes.ScopeInfo,
483: 	retAcl perms.ACL,
484: 	grantTuples []perms.GrantTuple,
485: 	retErr error,
486: ) {
487: 	const op = "auth.(verifier).performAuthCheck"
488: 	// Ensure we return an error by default if we forget to set this somewhere
489: 	retErr = errors.New(ctx, errors.Unknown, op, "default auth error", errors.WithoutEvent())
490: 	// Make the linter happy
491: 	_ = retErr
492: 	scopeInfo = new(scopes.ScopeInfo)
493: 
494: 	// This will always be set, so further down below we can switch on whether
495: 	// it's empty
496: 	userData.User.Id = util.Pointer(AnonymousUserId)
497: 
498: 	// Validate the token and fetch the corresponding user ID
499: 	switch v.requestInfo.TokenFormat {
500: 	case uint32(AuthTokenTypeUnknown):
501: 		// Nothing; remain as the anonymous user
502: 
503: 	case uint32(AuthTokenTypeRecoveryKms):
504: 		// We validated the encrypted token in decryptToken and handled the
505: 		// nonces there, so just set the user
506: 		userData.User.Id = util.Pointer("u_recovery")
507: 
508: 	case uint32(AuthTokenTypeBearer), uint32(AuthTokenTypeSplitCookie):
509: 		if v.requestInfo.Token == "" {
510: 			// This will end up staying as the anonymous user
511: 			break
512: 		}
513: 		tokenRepo, err := v.authTokenRepoFn()
514: 		if err != nil {
515: 			retErr = errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op)
516: 			return
517: 		}
518: 		at, err := tokenRepo.ValidateToken(v.ctx, v.requestInfo.PublicId, v.requestInfo.Token)
519: 		if err != nil {
520: 			// Continue as the anonymous user as maybe this token is expired but
521: 			// we can still perform the action
522: 			event.WriteError(ctx, op, err, event.WithInfoMsg("error validating token; continuing as anonymous user"))
523: 			break
524: 		}
525: 		if at != nil {
526: 			userData.Account.Id = util.Pointer(at.GetAuthAccountId())
527: 			userData.User.Id = util.Pointer(at.GetIamUserId())
528: 			if *userData.User.Id == "" {
529: 				event.WriteError(ctx, op, stderrors.New("perform auth check: valid token did not map to a user, likely because no account is associated with the user any longer; continuing as u_anon"), event.WithInfo("token_id", at.GetPublicId()))
530: 				userData.User.Id = util.Pointer(AnonymousUserId)
531: 				userData.Account.Id = nil
532: 			}
533: 		}
534: 	}
535: 
536: 	iamRepo, err := v.iamRepoFn()
537: 	if err != nil {
538: 		retErr = errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op, errors.WithMsg("failed to get iam repo"))
539: 		return
540: 	}
541: 
542: 	u, _, err := iamRepo.LookupUser(ctx, *userData.User.Id)
543: 	if err != nil {
544: 		retErr = errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op, errors.WithMsg("failed to lookup user"))
545: 		return
546: 	}
547: 	userData.User.Name = util.Pointer(u.Name)
548: 	userData.User.Email = util.Pointer(u.Email)
549: 	userData.User.FullName = util.Pointer(u.FullName)
550: 
551: 	if userData.Account.Id != nil && *userData.Account.Id != "" && v.passwordAuthRepoFn != nil && v.oidcAuthRepoFn != nil {
552: 		const domain = "auth"
553: 		var acct auth.Account
554: 		var err error
555: 		switch subtypes.SubtypeFromId(domain, *userData.Account.Id) {
556: 		case password.Subtype:
557: 			repo, repoErr := v.passwordAuthRepoFn()
558: 			if repoErr != nil {
559: 				retErr = errors.Wrap(ctx, repoErr, op, errors.WithMsg("failed to get password auth repo"))
560: 				return
561: 			}
562: 			acct, err = repo.LookupAccount(ctx, *userData.Account.Id)
563: 		case oidc.Subtype:
564: 			repo, repoErr := v.oidcAuthRepoFn()
565: 			if repoErr != nil {
566: 				retErr = errors.Wrap(ctx, repoErr, op, errors.WithMsg("failed to get oidc auth repo"))
567: 				return
568: 			}
569: 			acct, err = repo.LookupAccount(ctx, *userData.Account.Id)
570: 		default:
571: 			retErr = errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op, errors.WithMsg("unrecognized account id type"))
572: 			return
573: 		}
574: 		if err != nil {
575: 			if errors.IsNotFoundError(err) {
576: 				retErr = errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op, errors.WithMsg("account doesn't exist"))
577: 				return
578: 			}
579: 			retErr = errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op, errors.WithMsg("error looking up account"))
580: 			return
581: 		}
582: 		userData.Account.Name = util.Pointer(acct.GetName())
583: 		userData.Account.Email = util.Pointer(acct.GetEmail())
584: 		userData.Account.LoginName = util.Pointer(acct.GetLoginName())
585: 		userData.Account.Subject = util.Pointer(acct.GetSubject())
586: 	}
587: 
588: 	// Look up scope details to return. We can skip a lookup when using the
589: 	// global scope
590: 	switch v.res.ScopeId {
591: 	case scope.Global.String():
592: 		scopeInfo = &scopes.ScopeInfo{
593: 			Id:            scope.Global.String(),
594: 			Type:          scope.Global.String(),
595: 			Name:          scope.Global.String(),
596: 			Description:   "Global Scope",
597: 			ParentScopeId: "",
598: 		}
599: 
600: 	default:
601: 		scp, err := iamRepo.LookupScope(v.ctx, v.res.ScopeId)
602: 		if err != nil {
603: 			retErr = errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op)
604: 			return
605: 		}
606: 		if scp == nil {
607: 			retErr = errors.New(ctx, errors.InvalidParameter, op, fmt.Sprint("non-existent scope $q", v.res.ScopeId))
608: 			return
609: 		}
610: 		scopeInfo = &scopes.ScopeInfo{
611: 			Id:            scp.GetPublicId(),
612: 			Type:          scp.GetType(),
613: 			Name:          scp.GetName(),
614: 			Description:   scp.GetDescription(),
615: 			ParentScopeId: scp.GetParentId(),
616: 		}
617: 	}
618: 
619: 	// At this point we don't need to look up grants since it's automatically allowed
620: 	if v.requestInfo.TokenFormat == uint32(AuthTokenTypeRecoveryKms) {
621: 		aclResults.AuthenticationFinished = true
622: 		aclResults.Authorized = true
623: 		retErr = nil
624: 		return
625: 	}
626: 
627: 	var parsedGrants []perms.Grant
628: 
629: 	// Fetch and parse grants for this user ID (which may include grants for
630: 	// u_anon and u_auth)
631: 	grantTuples, err = iamRepo.GrantsForUser(v.ctx, *userData.User.Id)
632: 	if err != nil {
633: 		retErr = errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op)
634: 		return
635: 	}
636: 	parsedGrants = make([]perms.Grant, 0, len(grantTuples))
637: 	// Note: Below, we always skip validation so that we don't error on formats
638: 	// that we've since restricted, e.g. "id=foo;actions=create,read". These
639: 	// will simply not have an effect.
640: 	for _, pair := range grantTuples {
641: 		permsOpts := []perms.Option{
642: 			perms.WithUserId(*userData.User.Id),
643: 			perms.WithSkipFinalValidation(true),
644: 		}
645: 		if userData.Account.Id != nil {
646: 			permsOpts = append(permsOpts, perms.WithAccountId(*userData.Account.Id))
647: 		}
648: 		parsed, err := perms.Parse(
649: 			pair.ScopeId,
650: 			pair.Grant,
651: 			permsOpts...)
652: 		if err != nil {
653: 			retErr = errors.Wrap(ctx, err, op, errors.WithMsg(fmt.Sprintf("failed to parse grant %#v", pair.Grant)))
654: 			return
655: 		}
656: 		parsedGrants = append(parsedGrants, parsed)
657: 	}
658: 
659: 	retAcl = perms.NewACL(parsedGrants...)
660: 	aclResults = retAcl.Allowed(*v.res, v.act, *userData.User.Id)
661: 	// We don't set authenticated above because setting this but not authorized
662: 	// is used for further permissions checks, such as during recursive listing.
663: 	// So we want to make sure any code relying on that has the full set of
664: 	// grants successfully loaded.
665: 	aclResults.AuthenticationFinished = true
666: 	retErr = nil
667: 	return
668: }
669: 
670: // FetchActionSetForId returns the allowed actions for a given ID using the
671: // current set of ACLs and all other parameters the same (user, etc.)
672: func (r *VerifyResults) FetchActionSetForId(ctx context.Context, id string, availableActions action.ActionSet, opt ...Option) action.ActionSet {
673: 	return r.fetchActions(id, resource.Unknown, availableActions, opt...)
674: }
675: 
676: // FetchActionSetForType returns the allowed actions for a given collection type
677: // using the current set of ACLs and all other parameters the same (user, etc.)
678: func (r *VerifyResults) FetchActionSetForType(ctx context.Context, typ resource.Type, availableActions action.ActionSet, opt ...Option) action.ActionSet {
679: 	return r.fetchActions("", typ, availableActions, opt...)
680: }
681: 
682: func (r *VerifyResults) fetchActions(id string, typ resource.Type, availableActions action.ActionSet, opt ...Option) action.ActionSet {
683: 	switch {
684: 	case r.v.requestInfo.DisableAuthEntirely,
685: 		r.v.requestInfo.TokenFormat == uint32(AuthTokenTypeRecoveryKms):
686: 		// TODO: See if we can be better about what we return with the anonymous
687: 		// user and recovery KMS, perhaps given exclusionary options for each
688: 		return availableActions
689: 	}
690: 
691: 	// If there is no user ID set by definition there are no actions to fetch.
692: 	// This shouldn't happen because we should always fall back to at least the
693: 	// anonymous user so it's defense in depth.
694: 	if r.UserData.User.Id == nil {
695: 		return nil
696: 	}
697: 
698: 	opts := getOpts(opt...)
699: 	res := opts.withResource
700: 	// If not passed in, use what's already been populated through verification
701: 	if res == nil {
702: 		res = r.v.res
703: 	}
704: 	// If this is being called directly we may not have a resource yet
705: 	if res == nil {
706: 		res = new(perms.Resource)
707: 	}
708: 	if id != "" {
709: 		res.Id = id
710: 	}
711: 	if typ != resource.Unknown {
712: 		res.Type = typ
713: 	}
714: 
715: 	ret := make(action.ActionSet, 0, len(availableActions))
716: 	for _, act := range availableActions {
717: 		if r.v.acl.Allowed(*res, act, *r.UserData.User.Id).Authorized {
718: 			ret = append(ret, act)
719: 		}
720: 	}
721: 	if len(ret) == 0 {
722: 		return nil
723: 	}
724: 	return ret
725: }
726: 
727: func (r *VerifyResults) FetchOutputFields(res perms.Resource, act action.Type) perms.OutputFieldsMap {
728: 	switch {
729: 	case r.v.requestInfo.TokenFormat == uint32(AuthTokenTypeRecoveryKms):
730: 		return perms.OutputFieldsMap{"*": true}
731: 	case r.v.requestInfo.DisableAuthEntirely:
732: 		return nil
733: 	case r.UserData.User.Id == nil:
734: 		// If there is no user ID set by definition there are no actions to fetch.
735: 		// This shouldn't happen because we should always fall back to at least the
736: 		// anonymous user so it's defense in depth.
737: 		return nil
738: 	}
739: 
740: 	return r.v.acl.Allowed(res, act, *r.UserData.User.Id).OutputFields
741: }
742: 
743: // ACL returns the perms.ACL of the verifier.
744: func (r *VerifyResults) ACL() perms.ACL {
745: 	if r.v == nil {
746: 		return perms.ACL{}
747: 	}
748: 
749: 	return r.v.acl
750: }
751: 
752: // GetTokenFromRequest pulls the token from either the Authorization header or
753: // split cookies and parses it. If it cannot be parsed successfully, the issue
754: // is logged and we return blank, so logic will continue as the anonymous user.
755: // The public ID and _encrypted_ token are returned along with the token format.
756: func GetTokenFromRequest(ctx context.Context, kmsCache *kms.Kms, req *http.Request) (string, string, uint32) {
757: 	const op = "auth.GetTokenFromRequest"
758: 	// First, get the token, either from the authorization header or from split
759: 	// cookies
760: 	var receivedTokenType TokenFormat
761: 	var fullToken string
762: 	if authHeader := req.Header.Get("Authorization"); authHeader != "" {
763: 		headerSplit := strings.SplitN(strings.TrimSpace(authHeader), " ", 2)
764: 		if len(headerSplit) == 2 && strings.EqualFold(strings.TrimSpace(headerSplit[0]), "bearer") {
765: 			receivedTokenType = AuthTokenTypeBearer
766: 			fullToken = strings.TrimSpace(headerSplit[1])
767: 		}
768: 	}
769: 	if receivedTokenType != AuthTokenTypeBearer {
770: 		var httpCookiePayload string
771: 		var jsCookiePayload string
772: 		if hc, err := req.Cookie(handlers.HttpOnlyCookieName); err == nil {
773: 			httpCookiePayload = hc.Value
774: 		}
775: 		if jc, err := req.Cookie(handlers.JsVisibleCookieName); err == nil {
776: 			jsCookiePayload = jc.Value
777: 		}
778: 		if httpCookiePayload != "" && jsCookiePayload != "" {
779: 			receivedTokenType = AuthTokenTypeSplitCookie
780: 			fullToken = jsCookiePayload + httpCookiePayload
781: 		}
782: 	}
783: 
784: 	if receivedTokenType == AuthTokenTypeUnknown || fullToken == "" {
785: 		// We didn't find auth info or a client screwed up and put in a blank
786: 		// header instead of nothing at all, so return blank which will indicate
787: 		// the anonymous user
788: 		return "", "", uint32(AuthTokenTypeUnknown)
789: 	}
790: 
791: 	if strings.HasPrefix(fullToken, "r_") {
792: 		return "", fullToken, uint32(AuthTokenTypeRecoveryKms)
793: 	}
794: 
795: 	splitFullToken := strings.Split(fullToken, "_")
796: 	if len(splitFullToken) != 3 {
797: 		event.WriteError(ctx, op, stderrors.New("unexpected number of segments in token; continuing as anonymous user"), event.WithInfo("expected", 3, "found", len(splitFullToken)))
798: 		return "", "", uint32(AuthTokenTypeUnknown)
799: 	}
800: 
801: 	publicId := strings.Join(splitFullToken[0:2], "_")
802: 	encryptedToken := splitFullToken[2]
803: 
804: 	return publicId, encryptedToken, uint32(receivedTokenType)
805: }
806: 
807: // ScopesAuthorizedForList retrieves and returns all scopes where a user is authorized
808: // to perform a *list* action on. It looks recursively from `rootScopeId`.
809: func (r *VerifyResults) ScopesAuthorizedForList(ctx context.Context, rootScopeId string, resourceType resource.Type) (map[string]*scopes.ScopeInfo, error) {
810: 	const op = "auth.(VerifyResults).ScopesAuthorizedForList"
811: 
812: 	// Validation
813: 	switch {
814: 	case resourceType == resource.Unknown:
815: 		return nil, errors.New(ctx, errors.InvalidParameter, op, "unknown resource")
816: 	case r.v.iamRepoFn == nil:
817: 		return nil, errors.New(ctx, errors.InvalidParameter, op, "nil iam repo")
818: 	case rootScopeId == "":
819: 		return nil, errors.New(ctx, errors.InvalidParameter, op, "missing root scope id")
820: 	case r.Scope == nil:
821: 		return nil, errors.New(ctx, errors.InvalidParameter, op, "nil scope in auth results")
822: 	}
823: 
824: 	repo, err := r.v.iamRepoFn()
825: 	if err != nil {
826: 		return nil, err
827: 	}
828: 
829: 	// Get all scopes recursively. Start at global because we need to take into
830: 	// account permissions in parent scopes even if they want to scale back the
831: 	// returned information to a child scope and its children.
832: 	scps, err := repo.ListScopesRecursively(ctx, scope.Global.String())
833: 	if err != nil {
834: 		return nil, err
835: 	}
836: 
837: 	var deferredScopes []*iam.Scope
838: 	var globalHasList bool                                 // Store whether global has list permission
839: 	scopeResourceMap := make(map[string]*scopes.ScopeInfo) // Scope data per scope id
840: 
841: 	// For each scope, see if we have permission to list that resource in that scope
842: 	for _, scp := range scps {
843: 		scpId := scp.GetPublicId()
844: 		aSet := r.FetchActionSetForType(ctx,
845: 			resource.Unknown, // This is overridden by `WithResource` option.
846: 			action.ActionSet{action.List},
847: 			WithResource(&perms.Resource{Type: resourceType, ScopeId: scpId}),
848: 		)
849: 
850: 		// We only expect the action set to be nothing, or list. In case
851: 		// this is not the case, we bail out.
852: 		switch {
853: 		case len(aSet) == 0:
854: 			// Defer until we've read all scopes. We do this because if the
855: 			// ordering coming back isn't in parent-first ordering our map
856: 			// lookup might fail.
857: 			deferredScopes = append(deferredScopes, scp)
858: 		case len(aSet) == 1 || r.UserId == perms.RecoveryUserId:
859: 			if aSet[0] != action.List {
860: 				return nil, errors.New(ctx, errors.Internal, op, "unexpected action in set")
861: 			}
862: 			if scopeResourceMap[scpId] == nil {
863: 				scopeResourceMap[scpId] = &scopes.ScopeInfo{
864: 					Id:            scp.GetPublicId(),
865: 					Type:          scp.GetType(),
866: 					Name:          scp.GetName(),
867: 					Description:   scp.GetDescription(),
868: 					ParentScopeId: scp.GetParentId(),
869: 				}
870: 			}
871: 			if scpId == scope.Global.String() {
872: 				globalHasList = true
873: 			}
874: 		default:
875: 			return nil, errors.New(ctx, errors.Internal, op, "unexpected number of actions back in set")
876: 		}
877: 	}
878: 
879: 	// Now go through the deferred scopes and see if a parent matches
880: 	for _, scp := range deferredScopes {
881: 		// If they had list on global scope anything else is automatically
882: 		// included; otherwise if they had list on the parent scope, this
883: 		// scope is included in the map and is sufficient here.
884: 		if globalHasList || scopeResourceMap[scp.GetParentId()] != nil {
885: 			scpId := scp.GetPublicId()
886: 			if scopeResourceMap[scpId] == nil {
887: 				scopeResourceMap[scpId] = &scopes.ScopeInfo{
888: 					Id:            scp.GetPublicId(),
889: 					Type:          scp.GetType(),
890: 					Name:          scp.GetName(),
891: 					Description:   scp.GetDescription(),
892: 					ParentScopeId: scp.GetParentId(),
893: 				}
894: 			}
895: 		}
896: 	}
897: 
898: 	// Elide out any that aren't under the root scope id
899: 	elideScopes := make([]string, 0, len(scopeResourceMap))
900: 	for scpId, scp := range scopeResourceMap {
901: 		switch rootScopeId {
902: 		// If the root is global, it matches
903: 		case scope.Global.String():
904: 		// If the current scope matches the root, it matches
905: 		case scpId:
906: 			// Or if the parent of this scope is the root (for orgs that would mean
907: 			// a root scope ID which is covered in the case above, so this is really
908: 			// projects matching an org used as the root)
909: 		case scp.ParentScopeId:
910: 		default:
911: 			elideScopes = append(elideScopes, scpId)
912: 		}
913: 	}
914: 	for _, scpId := range elideScopes {
915: 		delete(scopeResourceMap, scpId)
916: 	}
917: 
918: 	// If we have nothing in scopeInfoMap at this point, we aren't authorized
919: 	// anywhere so return 403.
920: 	if len(scopeResourceMap) == 0 {
921: 		return nil, handlers.ForbiddenError()
922: 	}
923: 
924: 	return scopeResourceMap, nil
925: }
